verses.text: Who comes from Edom in crimson garments? It is the LORD, 'mighty to save,' who 'trodden the winepress alone.' He returns from judgment. The Redeemer who saves His people also treads down His enemies single-handed.

Who comes from Edom in crimson garments? It is the LORD, 'mighty to save,' who 'trodden the winepress alone.' He returns from judgment. The Redeemer who saves His people also treads down His enemies single-handed.

Who comes from Edom in crimson garments? It is the LORD, 'mighty to save,' who 'trodden the winepress alone.' He returns from judgment. The Redeemer who saves His people also treads down His enemies single-handed.

'The day of vengeance is in mine heart,' He says, having trodden the peoples in His anger, for none could help. Judgment is His work alone. The God who saves also avenges, and no power assists or hinders Him.

'The day of vengeance is in mine heart,' He says, having trodden the peoples in His anger, for none could help. Judgment is His work alone. The God who saves also avenges, and no power assists or hinders Him.

'The day of vengeance is in mine heart,' He says, having trodden the peoples in His anger, for none could help. Judgment is His work alone. The God who saves also avenges, and no power assists or hinders Him.

Isaiah recalls God's lovingkindnesses: 'in all their affliction he was afflicted,' and 'the angel of his presence saved them.' He shares His people's grief. The God of mercy feels their sorrows and carries them in love.

Isaiah recalls God's lovingkindnesses: 'in all their affliction he was afflicted,' and 'the angel of his presence saved them.' He shares His people's grief. The God of mercy feels their sorrows and carries them in love.

Isaiah recalls God's lovingkindnesses: 'in all their affliction he was afflicted,' and 'the angel of his presence saved them.' He shares His people's grief. The God of mercy feels their sorrows and carries them in love.
Yet they rebelled and 'vexed his holy Spirit,' so He turned against them; then He remembered the days of Moses. Their sin grieved Him. God's Spirit can be grieved, yet His memory of mercy returns.
Yet they rebelled and 'vexed his holy Spirit,' so He turned against them; then He remembered the days of Moses. Their sin grieved Him. God's Spirit can be grieved, yet His memory of mercy returns.
Yet they rebelled and 'vexed his holy Spirit,' so He turned against them; then He remembered the days of Moses. Their sin grieved Him. God's Spirit can be grieved, yet His memory of mercy returns.
He led them through the deep and gave them rest, 'to make thyself a glorious name.' His guidance won renown. God's tender leading of His people displays His glory to all.
He led them through the deep and gave them rest, 'to make thyself a glorious name.' His guidance won renown. God's tender leading of His people displays His glory to all.

Isaiah pleads, 'Look down from heaven'; 'doubtless thou art our father,' even when Abraham would not own them. He appeals to God's fatherhood. When all else fails, the believer claims God Himself as Father and Redeemer.

Isaiah pleads, 'Look down from heaven'; 'doubtless thou art our father,' even when Abraham would not own them. He appeals to God's fatherhood. When all else fails, the believer claims God Himself as Father and Redeemer.
'O LORD, why hast thou made us to err... return for thy servants' sake'; they feel almost cast off. He prays for God's return. The afflicted plead the bond of covenant when they feel forsaken.
